Depedencies:
Third-Party API Integrations: For auto-populating profile data (e.g., LinkedIn, AngelList).

Backend Infrastructure: For storing and managing rich profile data securely.

UI/UX Design: To create a seamless, engaging experience for users as they customize their profiles.
Feature Description: A comprehensive profile customization feature that allows both startups and CXOs to create rich, detailed profiles that showcase their skills, experience, engagement preferences, and more. This feature enables users to differentiate themselves and increases the accuracy of AI-driven matches. Users can integrate with third-party platforms like LinkedIn to auto-populate data, and they can continuously update and optimize their profiles to attract better matches.

Profile Customization Specs: Detailed specifications for profile sections and data inputs.

API Documentation: Integration details for third-party services (LinkedIn, AngelList).

Depedencies:
Data Model: User profile data is crucial for training and optimizing the matching algorithm.

ML Model: Initial model development and ongoing model training require a dedicated data science effort.

User Profiles: Rich, detailed user profiles with skills, preferences, and historical data are needed to feed the algorithm.
Feature Description: The AI-Driven Smart Matching Algorithm is the core feature of Strategix that intelligently matches startups with the most suitable CXOs based on multiple dimensions. This feature leverages advanced machine learning models to assess not just the traditional parameters like skills and experience, but also considers cultural fit, growth stage alignment, industry trends, and historical engagement success rates. The algorithm continuously evolves with a feedback loop that refines future matches based on user feedback and engagement outcomes.

The onboarding process will include a step-by-step guide, interactive tutorials, and progress tracking to minimize friction and maximize user engagement from the start.

Feature Name: CXO Role Rotation and Dynamic Team Building
Depedencies:
Backend API: For managing role rotations, timelines, and engagement transitions.

User Interface for Team Management: UX/UI to support adding, removing, and rotating CXOs seamlessly.

Notification System: To inform both startups and CXOs of role changes and transitions.
Feature Description: This feature allows startups to dynamically build their leadership teams by rotating CXOs in and out based on their evolving needs. Startups often face rapidly changing challenges as they grow, requiring different types of leadership expertise at different times. The CXO Role Rotation feature enables startups to set specific timeframes for fractional CXO roles (e.g., 3 months for a turnaround CFO, followed by 6 months of a growth-focused CMO), ensuring they always have the right expertise at the right time.
Related Documents:
Role Rotation Guidelines: Documentation on how startups can effectively rotate CXOs.
